recap: France dominated Sweden from start to finish, with Kylian Mbappé's brace extending his tournament tally to six goals and drawing level with Lionel Messi atop the scoring charts, while Bradley Barcola added a third to seal a 3-0 victory. Sweden, who finished third in their group, offered little resistance despite goalkeeper Jacob Widell Zetterström keeping the deficit manageable in a ragged first half, before France's superior quality on the counter-attack proved decisive after the break. The performance was overshadowed by an emotional moment when Mbappé ran to celebrate with manager Didier Deschamps, who had returned to the bench following the death of his mother.
